title: DORA TLPT — Test Planning Milestone and Scope Lock Audit Trail
exclusions: Scope refinement during threat intelligence gathering (expected methodology); competent authority review iterations; risk appetite alignment requiring multiple management body approvals.
evidence: TLPT planning evidence showing scope proposals, threat intelligence deliverables, scope lock sign-offs, competent authority correspondence, and risk parameter documentation.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.24 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.24 (Digital operational-resilience testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.25: DORA TLPT — Test Planning Milestone and Scope Lock Audit Trail.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.25' running on index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:milestone" (engagement_id, milestone, due_date, completed_date, scope_hash), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Log a test TLPT scope document 45 days past its planned lock date without formal sign-off; verify the audit trail search flags the unlocked scope with milestone details.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Log a test TLPT scope document locked and signed-off on the planned date; verify no scope-lock alert fires.

app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HEC from GRC
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:milestone" earliest=-730d
| eval due=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), done=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where milestone="scope_lock" AND (isnull(done) OR done>due)
| stats latest(scope_hash) as scope by engagement_id, milestone
| sort due
description: Threat-led penetration testing requires disciplined scope definition and change control. Tracking planning milestones and scope-lock timestamps shows the entity controlled test boundaries and prevented ad hoc scope creep that could invalidate results.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes tlpt:milestone. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Integrate CISO office project template; (2) alert 14 days before `due_date`; (3) store `scope_hash` of in-scope IPs/apps; (4) map engagement to competent authority reporting year; (5) restrict index to red-team and compliance roles. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tlpt:milestone`.
visualization: Gantt-style table, Single value (late milestones), Timeline.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Scope refinement iterations** — TLPT scope evolving during threat intelligence gathering is expected methodology; preliminary scope documents are not final.

2. **Competent authority scope review** — Supervisory feedback on scope proposals may require adjustments that extend the planning phase legitimately.

3. **Critical function boundary determination** — Mapping critical functions to specific ICT systems for targeting requires iterative validation with business stakeholders.

4. **Tester qualification verification** — Verifying Art.27 tester qualifications and conflict-of-interest clearances adds administrative time to scope lock.

5. **Risk appetite alignment** — Management body review of test risk parameters (production impact limits, data handling) may require multiple approval cycles.

**Operational suppression:** Maintain a lookup table (`dora_approved_exceptions.csv`) mapping known legitimate activities by process owner, approval reference, and expiry date. Filter the SPL results against this lookup to suppress documented exceptions and reduce alert fatigue. Review and rotate entries quarterly per the ICT risk management framework review cycle.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `grc` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.24`. The control objective is: "Evidence that DORA Art.24 (Digital operational-resilience testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.25: DORA TLPT — Test Planning Milestone and Scope Lock Audit Trail." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `grc` under `tlpt:milestone`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Integrate CISO office project template. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** alert 14 days before `due_date`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** store `scope_hash` of in-scope IPs/apps. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** map engagement to competent authority reporting year. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

• **Hint 5:** restrict index to red-team and compliance roles. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

This search aggregates `latest()` across the last 730 days, grouped by `engagement_id, milestone`. For "DORA TLPT — Test Planning Milestone and Scope Lock Audit Trail," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the DORA control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time. `earliest()` and `latest()` extract the first and last value per group — typically used to measure durations ('first seen' to 'last seen') or to pick the freshest attribute value when multiple events update the same entity.

Why aggregate at search time rather than rely on raw events: compliance reporting requires summarisation to be defensible and stable. A raw-event dashboard can show different counts depending on the exact second it was queried; an aggregated result rounded to the UC's schedule produces consistent numbers across the reporting period. If the downstream consumer (GRC platform, regulator submission) expects stable daily/weekly figures, schedule this search to write to a summary index and read from the summary for reports — that way the numbers quoted on Monday match what was quoted on Wednesday.

Cardinality vigilance: if `engagement_id, milestone` has very high cardinality (e.g. grouping by a free-text `user_agent` or `url` field), the result set explodes and the search becomes slow. Check cardinality with `| stats dc(engagement_id) as cardinality` before scheduling; if >100000 distinct groups, either narrow the search, pre-filter to the top N values, or redesign the search to aggregate at a coarser grain.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.3.25: DORA TLPT — Test Planning Milestone and Scope Lock Audit Tra]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -730d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.3.25,reg=DORA
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=tlpt:milestone earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `due`, `engagement_id`, `milestone`, `scope`. Run:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=tlpt:milestone earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table due, engagement_id, milestone, scope
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.3.25: DORA evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Gantt-Style Table (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Single-value KPI (`late milestones`):** Place at top-left. Configure `underLabel` with the regulation clause (e.g. 'DORA current violation count') and set a red colour range for any non-zero value. A zero reading is the steady state for a healthy control.

**Panel 3 — Time trend (the key fields):** Place in the main content row. Use `timechart span=1d` aggregated appropriately for the regulation's review cadence (daily for PCI/HIPAA, weekly for ISO-style controls). Annotate the chart with control-change dates so auditors can see when mitigations took effect.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**DORA-specific failure modes:**

• Third-party ICT risk register (Art. 28): DORA requires a Register of Information listing every critical ICT third-party service provider. Detections touching supplier risk must reference this register; if a supplier appears in telemetry but not in the Register, that is a Register-completeness finding, not just a technical anomaly.

• Testing cadence (Art. 24-27): DORA's TLPT (Threat-Led Penetration Testing) is required every 3 years for significant institutions. Detections measuring resilience testing must differentiate TLPT (supervisor-led) from self-assessment — mixing them dilutes the evidence.

• Incident classification thresholds (Art. 18): DORA uses specific criteria for 'major' vs 'significant' incidents — client impact, duration, data volume. Detections must classify against these specific thresholds, which differ from GDPR breach criteria; a single event can be major-DORA but not a personal-data breach.

• Resilience testing record retention (Art. 27): results of digital operational resilience tests must be retained. Align log retention to match — typically 5+ years. Verify `frozenTimePeriodInSecs >= 157680000` on any index named by a DORA resilience-testing UC.
grandmaExplanation: We threat-led penetration testing requires disciplined scope definition and change control.
